exemplar.title: This investigation aims to determine the effect of how changing the concentration(0.010g/100g of soil, 0.050g/100g of soil, 0.150g/100g of soil, 0.500g/100g of soil, 1.000g/100g of soil,) of microplastics in soil affects the concentration of chlorophyll a and b in Common Oats (Avena sativa) calculated through the measurement of the absorbance of the chlorophyll extract at 645 nm and 663 nm from a spectrophotometer.

detailedExemplarData.title: This investigation aims to determine the effect of how changing the concentration(0.010g/100g of soil, 0.050g/100g of soil, 0.150g/100g of soil, 0.500g/100g of soil, 1.000g/100g of soil,) of microplastics in soil affects the concentration of chlorophyll a and b in Common Oats (Avena sativa) calculated through the measurement of the absorbance of the chlorophyll extract at 645 nm and 663 nm from a spectrophotometer.

subject.title: Biology
subject.description:
**IB Biology** is a **Group 4** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **four interconnected themes: Unity and Diversity, Form and Function, Interaction and Interdependence, and Continuity and Change**, with a strong focus on **experimental work, scientific inquiry, data analysis, and practical investigation skills**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Multiple-choice and data-based questions | 1 hour 20 min | 2 hours | 36% |
| **Paper 2** | Short and extended response questions | 1 hour 30 min | 2 hours 30 min | 44% |
| **Scientific Investigation** | Individual research project | 10 hours | 10 hours | 20% |

strengths: Research question is embedded within a specific and well-justified context linking local plastic pollution to chlorophyll synthesis.
Methodological choices (particle size range, mixing protocol, spectrophotometer settings) are clearly explained with linkages to equipment constraints and literature.
Detailed extraction and measurement steps, including an innovative layered mixing protocol, enhance reproducibility.
weaknesses: Minor ambiguities remain (lighting intensity unquantified, soil composition undefined) that prevent fully unambiguous reproduction.
Some procedural uncertainties (pipette, balance) are mentioned but not quantified or accounted for.
A few areas of text lack clarity (abrupt sentence on plant selection; extraneous mathematical notation).
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments place this criterion solidly in the 5â6 band for context and methodological explanation, while methodology description shows minor omissions equating to band 3â4. Balancing these yields a holistic score of 5, reflecting a strong design with a few reproducibility gaps.

strengths: Data recording and processing are communicated both clearly and precisely, with well-labelled tables and appropriate significant figures.
Comprehensive raw data table supports precise tracking of trials and uncertainties.
Data processing (concentration calculations, means, SD, graph) is carried out accurately with no major errors.
weaknesses: Instrument uncertainties are listed but never propagated into concentration calculations, weakening quantitative rigor.
Discussion conflates natural variability (SD) with measurement uncertainty, causing conceptual ambiguity.
Qualitative observations are not integrated with quantitative trends, missing an opportunity to deepen analysis.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Communication of data is at the top of the 5â6 band, uncertainty consideration matches band 3â4 and processing quality falls in band 3â4. A balanced, conservative holistic score of 4 reflects strengths in clarity and accuracy alongside significant omissions in uncertainty treatment.

strengths: Conclusion directly addresses the research question and is fully consistent with the observed decreasing chlorophyll trend.
Justification is coherent and links experimental results to the stated hypothesis.
weaknesses: The conclusion is truncated mid-argument, leaving some justification incomplete.
Comparison to the broader scientific context is limited to a single micro-algae study with minimal depth.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Relevance and consistency with analysis place the conclusion in band 5â6, while scientific context comparison sits in band 3â4. A holistic score of 4 fairly balances a strong core conclusion with limited contextual breadth and presentation gaps.

strengths: Specific methodological weaknesses (uneven mixing, plant variability, pot crowding) are clearly identified and linked to data reliability.
Improvements proposed (increased trials, mechanical mixing) are realistic and target the stated limitations.
weaknesses: The relative impact of each specific weakness is described but not quantified, reducing depth of evaluation.
Improvement explanations lack detail on how proposed changes would quantitatively reduce error or variability.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Methodological weaknesses are described in band 3â4 and improvements also in band 3â4. Awarding a score of 4 reflects a solid evaluation with room for deeper impact analysis and explanatory detail.

strandSelections.reasoning: The concluding statement directly addresses the research question and is fully consistent with the decreasing chlorophyll trend shown in processed data. Justification is coherent, though not mathematically rigorous, warranting band 5-6 with a score of 5.

Raw and processed data are presented in clearly labelled tables with units and appropriate significant figures; calculations and a graph are included. Minor typographical issues do not obscure comprehension, so communication is **both clear and precise** (band 5-6, score 5).

Data are converted to chlorophyll concentrations with correct formulae, means and standard deviations are calculated, and a trend graph produced. Some claims about exponential decline and variability are not statistically substantiated, but **major errors are absent**. Consequently a high 3-4 band score of 4 is justified.

Specific weaknesses (uneven MP distribution, plant natural variability, pot crowding) are identified and their likely influence on data reliability is described, though the magnitude of impact is not fully explained. This matches band 3-4 with a higher score of 4.

The student compares results to one published micro-algae study and notes concentration differences, providing **some relevant comparison** but limited depth or breadth. This fits band 3-4; a mid score of 3 is appropriate.

The research question is embedded in a detailed discussion of local plastic pollution in Yangon, the physiology of chlorophyll and photosynthesis, and previously published findings on micro-plastics. This constitutes a **specific and appropriate context** rather than a mere outline. Minor digressions do not detract significantly, so the performance aligns with the upper band (5-6); a mid-band score of 5 is appropriate.

Instrument uncertainties are listed for balances, pipettes, etc., but these are **not propagated** into processed results. Standard deviation is discussed yet conflated with uncertainty. Evidence of consideration exists, but omissions are significant, matching band 3-4; a conservative score of 3 is awarded.

Improvementsâmore trials and mechanical soil mixingâare **realistic and target stated weaknesses**, but explanations of how they would reduce specific errors are brief. This sits solidly in band 3-4; a score of 3 is given.

The student **explains** why each design choice (MP mass range, layered mixing, spectrophotometer wavelengths, exponential series) is necessary for obtaining relevant and sufficient data. Linkages to equipment constraints and literature justify decisions, demonstrating explanation rather than simple description. Some aspects (e.g., seed overcrowding) are not anticipated beforehand, so a 5 rather than 6 is awarded.

Step-by-step instructions list quantities, timings, wavelengths, and formulae, allowing another investigator to reproduce the work with only **minor ambiguities** (lighting intensity not quantified, soil composition undefined). This satisfies the 3-4 band; clarity is strong enough for the upper score of 4.

annotations.comment: The expectation drawn from a microalgae study is not fully justified for a terrestrial plant system. Provide literature support for extrapolating from algae to oats.
The variables table omits clear headers for dependent and control variables. Ensure all variable tables follow a consistent labelling format.
The alternate hypothesis is well justified, clearly linking increased microplastic concentrations to reduced chlorophyll via nutrient uptake limitations.
Defining microplastics by size and justifying the sieve choice demonstrates clear methodological consideration for ensuring relevant particle sizing.

The research question is embedded within a specific context linking microplastic levels in soil to photosynthesis impact in Common Oats, demonstrating a strong understanding of the local environmental significance.
The sentence introducing plant selection ends abruptly (âand the other choices made beforehandâ), leaving ambiguity. Complete this thought to clarify rationale for choosing Common Oats.
Consider refining the research question wording for clarity by simplifying the list of concentrations and ensuring proper punctuation around parenthetical items.
The theory list includes an unrelated mathematical notation (\(\mathbb{Z}^{\mathbb{Z}}\)) that distracts from the biological explanations. Remove or correct this formatting error.
Link each theoretical mechanism more explicitly to chlorophyll synthesis processes to strengthen the rationale for the variable selection.
The pipette and balance uncertainties are mentioned but not quantified in the procedure. Specify how these instrument uncertainties will be minimized or accounted for during data collection.
The layered mixing protocol is innovative and enhances uniform distribution of microplastics, which supports reproducibility.
The mixing diagram would benefit from a scale bar or measurement annotation to clarify the layer thickness and improve protocol clarity.
The detailed extraction and measurement steps greatly enhance procedural reproducibility and clarity.
Include a step to record blank cuvette absorbance before each measurement to ensure calibration consistency in the data processing.
Ethical and environmental considerations are appropriately addressed, showing awareness of safety and disposal impacts.
The equipment list lacks uncertainty values for critical mass measurements of microplastics, which affects reproducibility of sample preparation.
The equipment table lists many items but does not highlight which uncertainties are most critical for data quality. Prioritize reporting uncertainties for spectrophotometer and balance readings.
Fixed pot placement against a single window may introduce positional bias. Randomize pot positions daily to control for light intensity variation.
The synthesis equations for chlorophyll a and b are merged in a single line, making them hard to parse. Separate each formula and define each variable clearly using $$...$$.
The raw data table is comprehensive, listing each trial with uncertainties, which supports clear and precise data recording.
The qualitative traits summary is thorough but not integrated with quantitative trends. Discuss how stem color or twisting correlates with measured chlorophyll values.
The limitations are specific and explain how uneven mixing, natural plant variability, and small sample size affect data reliability.
Consider employing a mechanical mixer or standardized device to ensure homogeneous microplastic distribution and discuss the feasibility in a school lab context.
Instrument uncertainties are stated but never propagated into chlorophyll concentration calculations. Include uncertainty propagation to strengthen quantitative analysis.
Descriptive statistics summarise distribution effectively, showing skew, mean, and standard deviation across treatments.
Standard deviation discussion conflates natural variability with measurement uncertainty. Clarify these concepts and separate uncertainty analysis from variation interpretation.
The interpretation linking decreasing absorbance at both wavelengths to the hypothesis demonstrates a clear and consistent conclusion.
Plotting error bars on the chlorophyll concentration graph would visualise data variability and enhance the precision of the data presentation.
The conclusion section is truncated mid-sentence; ensure the full concluding argument is presented to maintain consistency with the analysis.
Stating increased replicates as an improvement is realistic, but explain how additional trials quantitatively reduce variability to make the suggestion more actionable.

strands.content: The communication of the recording and processing of the data is either clear or precise.

strands.name: Consideration of uncertainties
strands.content: The recording and processing of data shows evidence of a consideration of uncertainties but with some significant omissions or inaccuracies.

strands.name: Data processing quality

strands.name: Consideration of uncertainties

strands.name: Conclusion relevance and consistency
strands.content: A conclusion is described that is relevant to the research question but is not fully consistent with the analysis presented.

strands.name: Scientific context comparison

strands.name: Conclusion relevance and consistency
strands.content: A conclusion is justified that is relevant to the research question and fully consistent with the analysis presented.

strands.name: Scientific context comparison
strands.content: A conclusion is justified through relevant comparison to the accepted scientific context.

strands.name: Improvements

strands.name: Methodological weaknesses
strands.content: The report describes specific methodological weaknesses or limitations.

strands.name: Improvements
strands.content: Realistic improvements to the investigation that are relevant to the identified weaknesses or limitations, are described.

strands.name: Methodological weaknesses

strands.name: Improvements
